cityDNA.coreFantasy: A merfolk harbor-basin city where canal halls, cargo yards, public works, nursery pools, harbor basilicas, and coral-lyre rites make the coast sing as civic infrastructure.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Thalassmere is not generic underwater beauty; the uploaded images show a coast-facing merfolk city of canals, logistics, public works, nurseries, and harbor ritual.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Thalassmere turns tide sorcery, shell resonance, canal halls, cargo order, survey authority, spawning care, and harbor worship into a maritime civic machine.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Pale marble, coral stone, seashell, mother-of-pearl, canal halls, shell spiral windows, cargo yards, survey halls, nursery basins, harbor arches, bridgelets, and coral lyre forms.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Merfolk culture organized around tide law, canal life, cargo logistics, spawning protection, public works, and musical harbor ritual.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Coral lyres, deep choirs, shell spirals, canal pillars, blue lamps, gold seals, shell inlay, spawning pools, harbor arches, and tide-seal citadels symbolize water as law and song.

canonLocks: Thalassmere is a mature merfolk harbor-basin city, not generic underwater ornament.
The Wonder is Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir.
The Beacon is Canal Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five primary districts are Canal Hall, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, and Harbor Campus.

Exact uploaded anchors include S1 Canal Hall, Canal Hall Beacon, S1 Canal Pillar, S1 Canal Kiosk, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Thalassmere's strongest civic read is deep choir splendor shaped into institutions.
The uploaded image and sidecar pass were folded into v2 fields without adding a new top-level image-analysis section.
rejectedReads: coastal scenery with no civic water systems
harbor ritual separated from public works
cautionFlags: Preserve exact uploaded sidecar anchor names, especially S1 Canal Hall / Pillar / Kiosk and S5 Harbor Campus / Arch / Bridgelet.
Keep canal, cargo, survey, nursery, and harbor systems visible together.
The city is coast/harbor-basin civic fantasy, not only underwater spectacle.

environment.environmentalLogic: Water is street, voice, border, nursery, logistics route, and sacred medium. Thalassmere turns the harbor basin into a civic instrument.

forms: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir
skyline: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir as far wonder anchor
Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel as central authority
Canal Hall Beacon as water-route signal
harbor basilica, cargo yards, survey halls, and nursery basins around the harbor basin

civilization.people: Merfolk citizens move through coast, canal, and plaza as civic participants: canal workers, cargo clerks, surveyors, nursery keepers, harbor clergy, and tide-law officials.
civilization.governance: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel governs canals, cargo seals, public works permits, nursery protections, harbor rites, and tide-law petitions.
civilization.religion: Deep Choir rites, coral lyre ceremonies, harbor basilica blessings, shell resonance, and spawning-pool protections make water sacred and administrative.

streets.appearance: Pearl-bright canal streets connect S1 Canal Hall, cargo yards, survey halls, nursery basins, and harbor basilica arches, with water moving through every public district.
streets.movement: Movement follows canals, bridgelets, docks, seawalls, survey yards, cargo routes, nursery edges, and harbor campus plazas.
streets.lighting: Water-reflected daylight and pearl shimmer dominate by day; blue lamps and harbor lanterns guide night routes.
streets.weatherEffects: Tide shifts, sea spray, harbor fog, and reef glare affect every route and make public works critical.
streets.dayCycle: Canal operations, cargo movement, survey work, nursery care, and harbor crossings dominate daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Blue canal lamps, quiet nursery pools, harbor basilica rites, and bridgelet watch dominate night.

housing.wealthy: Water-facing homes near the Tide-Seal Citadel or harbor basilica, with shell spiral windows and private basin access.
housing.middle: Cargo, survey, and canal-family homes along bridgelets, docks, and canal plazas.
housing.poor: Harbor-edge rooms and service courts near cargo yards, public water, and canal maintenance routes.

districts.name: Canal Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Canal coordination, public signal, water-route access, and civic orientation.
districts.visualIdentity: S1 Canal Hall, Canal Hall Beacon, S1 Canal Pillar, S1 Canal Kiosk.

districts.name: Warehousing / Logistics
districts.civicPurpose: Cargo movement, inventory, dock accountability, and harbor supply.
districts.visualIdentity: Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor engineering, survey, permit control, and water-route maintenance.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.

districts.name: Nursery / Spawning Grounds
districts.civicPurpose: Protected spawning, early life care, family continuity, and communal vulnerability.
districts.visualIdentity: Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor ritual, public crossing, maritime worship, and civic-water identity.
districts.visualIdentity: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet.

wonder.name: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir
wonder.symbolicRole: Song, tide, coral, civic memory, and merfolk identity made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Far skyline basin of coral lyre forms, pearl shimmer, water courts, and deep choir amphitheater geometry.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide rites, deep choir assemblies, harbor ceremonies, and public memory.
storyHooks: A coral lyre note changes the tide before the surveyors can explain it.

beacon.name: Canal Hall Beacon
beacon.visualRole: Beacon set into S1 Canal Hall, with canal pillar, kiosk, and water-route approach.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates canal movement, cargo routes, public works, nursery protection, and harbor ritual response.
storyHooks: A Beacon change makes every canal route choose between cargo and nursery safety.

seatOfPower.name: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Tide law, shell memory, and merfolk authority made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Water-facing citadel with shell spiral windows, sealed chambers, public terraces, and harbor sightlines.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs canal law, cargo seals, public works permits, spawning protections, and harbor rites.
storyHooks: A shell spiral window frames the tide law everyone misread.

majorLandmarks.name: Cargo Yard
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Cargo court with gold-seal monument, inventory nook, crates, ropes, and dock platforms.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Cargo movement, inventory control, and supply accountability.
storyHooks: A gold-seal inventory record proves the harbor moved more than goods.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Public works hall with blue-lamp monument, permit yard, gauges, maps, and water access.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Surveys, permits, engineering, and harbor maintenance.
storyHooks: A permit yard map reveals the reef has grown into the city’s legal boundary.

majorLandmarks.name: Nursery Basin
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Protected basin with shell inlay, spawning pool nook, soft light, and family routes.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Spawning protection, family continuity, and communal care.
storyHooks: A spawning pool change makes the harbor ceremony impossible to ignore.

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Basilica with harbor arch, bridgelet, piers, signal lanterns, and coral-shell detail.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Blessings, crossings, public rites, mourning, and harbor identity.
storyHooks: A bridgelet crossing becomes a rite of citizenship.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show S1 Canal Hall, Canal Hall Beacon, S1 Canal Pillar, and S1 Canal Kiosk.
S2 must show Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, and Inventory Hall Nook.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, and Permit Yard Nook.
S4 must show Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, and Spawning Pool Nook.
S5 must show S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir, Canal Hall Beacon, and Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel legible together.
Use pale marble, coral stone, seashell, mother-of-pearl, canal halls, cargo yards, survey halls, nursery basins, harbor arches, and bridgelets.
requiredVisualElements: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir
